summary.intent: Address Codex review feedback on PR #731 (field-form-adapter-retrofit-audit) by fixing two inline review comments, resolving those threads, and then iterating on a third Codex finding about an overly broad StatusRegion detector in the Dioxus form adapter.
summary.outcome: All three Codex review issues were fixed and committed (commits 7f75fd2bd and the 'Narrow Dioxus form status region detection' commit), CI passed fully after a transient unit test failure was cleared via job rerun, and the PR was left in a clean, all-green state awaiting final Codex approval.

repo: Unit tests are run via `cargo xtest unit` which maps to a nextest workspace split defined in xtask/src/test.rs
CI workflow uses a multi-stage matrix; Release Verification depends only on clippy (not unit tests), so it can run independently
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as an issue comment on the PR

The Dioxus form adapter's StatusRegion child detector was matching any component named `StatusRegion`, not just the form part's own StatusRegion — needed narrowing to the fully-qualified form part path
The `has_status_region` signal on form::Root was added to expose status region presence to wrapper components, with an SSR regression test for the wrapped component case

code.finding: Direct-child StatusRegion detector was too broad — matched any child component named `StatusRegion` rather than specifically the form adapter's StatusRegion part, causing false suppression of the fallback live region

Regression test added: `form_status_region_detector_ignores_unrelated_status_region_components` verifies that an unrelated child component named StatusRegion does not suppress the fallback live region
Release Verification job depends only on clippy, not on unit tests — confirmed by grep on `release:` needs section
Unit test stage dispatches via `Stage::Unit` through cargo nextest; the exact reproducer command is `cargo xtest unit`

workflow: `gh run view --log` withholds logs while the overall workflow run is still in progress, even if the specific job has failed — use `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/jobs/{job_id}/logs` as a fallback
GitHub job log blobs (via actions/jobs API) may return 404 for several minutes after a job fails; polling with sleep is necessary
`gh run rerun --failed` reruns only the failed jobs in a workflow, not the entire matrix
GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation requires the thread node ID (PRRT_... format), not the comment database ID
When a CI failure does not reproduce locally with the exact same command, treat it as a transient runner issue and use `gh run rerun --failed` rather than making code changes
Codex review threads can be resolved via GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation; inline replies use `gh api .../pulls/{n}/comments` with `in_reply_to` parameter

friction: GitHub `gh run view --log` refuses to return logs while the parent workflow is still in_progress, forcing use of the lower-level jobs API
Job log blobs via `gh api .../actions/jobs/{id}/logs` returned 404 for an extended period after the job failed, requiring multiple retries with sleeps
GraphQL query had syntax errors (extra closing brace) that required several iterations to correct
GraphQL status query hit the network sandbox repeatedly, requiring escalation approvals
Codex has not yet posted a final approval reaction/comment after the last review trigger, leaving merge state unclear at session end
Release Verification remained in a long-running pending state due to release-profile compilation, causing confusion about whether it was stuck or progressing
open_items: Awaiting Codex final approval reaction or review comment after the `@codex review` trigger posted at the end of the session
PR #731 is not yet merged — all CI is green but review decision status was not confirmed as approved at session end
No log was ever retrieved for the original transient unit failure (job 81769492448) — root cause remains unknown and unconfirmed as truly transient
